metre, but in a footnote of the Pingalacchandas the author of the

LXX. 18 and in another place. He gives entirely new names to
certain metres : He gives a romantic name, Vildsint, to GIV. 32,
which is found neither in Pingala nor in the Vrttaraindkara. He calls
the metre Harini by the name Vr$abhacarita (GIV. 10). All these
things suggest that Varaha had a more ancient source like
which must have been more thorough than either. In XIV. 6 (I
half) out of 30 mdtrds he has used 22 short syllables, thereby showing
his mastery over metrics. In XXIV, 13, 14 he employs the Pufpi-
tagrd metre with telling effect. He makes use of the Arydgili rarely
as in XGV, 15, 33 andLVIlI. 58. In a large number of instances
